title: tetraria bromoides
intro: Tetraria bromoides, also known as the broom fern or broom sedge, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Restionaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Tetraria bromoides include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and treat respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Tetraria bromoides is traditionally prepared as a decoction, where the plant's leaves and stems are boiled in water to create a medicinal tea, or as a poultice to apply directly to the affected area.
